{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks which acid does not show coagulation necrosis on contact. From the provided documents, Document [26] (Title: Alkali Toxicity(Archived) -- Pathophysiology) states that acids with a pH of less than 7.0 induce coagulative necrosis. HCl (HC1) and H2SO4 (H,SO4) are strong acids with pH values less than 7.0, so they would cause coagulative necrosis. HF (Hydrofluoric acid) is noted in Document [11] (Title: Hydrofluoric Acid Burns -- Pathophysiology) to cause liquefactive necrosis rather than coagulative necrosis. HNO3 (Nitric acid) is a strong acid but does not specifically mention coagulative necrosis in the provided documents. Given the information, HF is the acid that does not show coagulation necrosis on contact.", "answer_choice": "C"}
